title: The devil may wear Prada on Earth, but NASA Artemis astronauts will on the moon. Here's why.
summary: NASA's Artemis astronauts will wear Prada-designed spacesuits on the moon, highlighting a collaboration between fashion and space exploration. The partnership aims to enhance suit functionality and aesthetics for lunar missions.

title: 'Battlestar Galactica: Scattered Hopes' is a relentlessly oppressive roguelite inspired by FTL
summary: Battlestar Galactica: Scattered Hopes is a roguelite game inspired by FTL, featuring relentless pursuit by the Cylons. Survivors must rejoin the Battlestar Galactica to escape in this brutal game.

title: The Canon EOS R5 Mark II is our choice as the best-resolution Canon camera – it's perfect for astrophotography and is $501 off in this Walmart deal
summary: The Canon EOS R5 Mark II is praised as the best-resolution Canon camera for astrophotography and is currently discounted by $501 at Walmart. Expert tester James Abbott gave it a flawless review, highlighting its impressive performance and all-round capabilities.

title: Mars orbiter captures striking images of 'chaos and craters' carved by ancient floods
summary: ESA's Mars Express orbiter captured dramatic images of a rugged Martian landscape shaped by ancient catastrophic floods billions of years ago. The orbiter revealed striking views of 'chaos and craters' carved by these ancient floods.

title: I thought my backyard had too much light pollution for astrophotography. This telescope proved me wrong
summary: A telescope designed for backyard use, the Vaonis Vespera Pro, enables detailed deep-space astrophotography despite light pollution. The device proved effective even in areas with streetlights and terraced houses.

title: Auroras over Australia look like sci-fi from space | Space photo of the day for May 15, 2026
summary: An astronaut aboard the International Space Station captured stunning images of auroras over Australia, displaying vibrant green and pink hues. The space photo was featured as the 'Space photo of the day' for May 15, 2026.

title: James Webb Space Telescope maps our universe's largest structure in unprecedented detail
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to map the universe's largest structure, the 'cosmic web' of galaxies, with unprecedented detail.

title: 4 bright stars form a giant 'diamond' in the May night sky: Here's how to find it
summary: The article describes a celestial event in May's night sky where four bright stars form a giant 'diamond' shape, known as the Great Diamond of Spring. It also mentions the galaxies within this formation.

title: The 'Doctor Who' TV movie at 30: Too British for America, too American for the UK
summary: A retrospective on the 30th anniversary of the 'Doctor Who' TV movie, highlighting its mixed reception in both the UK and US due to its transatlantic production challenges. The film attempted to revive the iconic Time Lord character but faced cultural and stylistic mismatches.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
summary: NASA's 34th SpaceX resupply mission launched to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments. The mission lifted off on a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.

title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover successfully removed a stuck rock from the surface after drilling a sample. The rock detached after engineers repositioned the arm and used vibration techniques.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. The galaxy's face is partially obscured by reddish-brown dust filaments, with background galaxies visible in red, blue, and orange hues.

title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
summary: A recent analysis identified regions where artificial light at night has increased and decreased, offering new insights into Earth's nighttime illumination. This study highlights changes in global light pollution patterns.

title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
summary: NASA has issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) to collaborate with industry for the Mars Telecommunications Network, aiming to enable reliable, high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will utilize high-performance orbiters to support surface, orbital, and human exploration.

title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
summary: NASA is partnering with Eta Space to test cryogenic fuel management technologies in orbit through the LOXSAT mission, aiming to enable future in-space propellant depots for deep space missions.

title: Fresh Food Delivery for Space Station
summary: NASA astronauts and ESA astronaut enjoyed fresh food delivered by Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station. The shipment included oranges, apples, onions, and other fresh produce.

title: Ice Moves Out of Aniak
summary: Ice jams and flooding occurred along Alaska’s Kuskokwim River due to spring melt. The event highlights the impact of seasonal changes on local communities.

title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
summary: NASA-supported 3D printing technology developed for lunar habitats is now being used by Branch Technology Inc. to create efficient and strong building structures on Earth. The company's Freeform 3D Printing process uses lightweight lattice structures for construction.

title: Rise Goes to Washington
summary: The Artemis II zero gravity indicator, named 'Rise,' was displayed during a congressional meeting in Washington. NASA astronauts and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen discussed the mission with congressional staff.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones during this period.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the International Space Station (ISS) on its third attempt. The launch occurred just over two weeks after the Progress MS-34 spacecraft docked with the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are set to conduct nine launches during the week of May 11, making for a busy launch schedule. The launches span multiple locations in the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful 33-engine static fire test. The event marks a significant milestone for the partner involved.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch both cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making progress toward reusability in spaceflight.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work, with Flight 12 approaching. The process follows the conclusion of Flight 11 six months prior.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before a busier launch schedule.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard Falcon 9, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the news, but no specific details about the space industry are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and validating materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas from these early-career scientists.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute deployment test was conducted, likely to ensure safe landing systems. The test involved baking or heating the parachute to simulate re-entry conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars landing, undergoing rigorous sterilization to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone to prevent microbial contamination. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life on Mars while adhering to strict planetary protection measures.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris. The forum aims to gather feedback from small and medium-sized enterprises on topics affecting the space sector.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications capabilities. The move aims to provide integrated and resilient deep-space communication solutions for customers.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services through space-based VHF communications. The deal aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and tracking.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are seeking infrastructure financing to scale their operations, highlighting a gap in growth-stage capital within the continent's space industry.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for the missile-defense program, stating it does not accurately reflect what they are building. The program is part of the U.S. missile defense efforts.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company, along with mentorship and support for startups in the defense space market and government contracting.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined the preliminary plan for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch to test technologies in low Earth orbit for a future Moon landing in 2028.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking a significant milestone for the company. This event introduces a new player in the space launch industry.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the cost of the Golden Dome program at $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by Gen. Michael Guetlein in March.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to send an automated mini lab to the International Space Station (ISS). The device, designed for orbital high-throughput screening, will be installed in standard mid-deck lockers aboard crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff but left after Administrator Jared Isaacman's appointment. His new role is seen as a political move within the space agency.

title: What Will the Golden Dome Cost? Gen. Guetlein Pushes Back on CBO Cost Estimate
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program disputed a Congressional Budget Office (CBO) cost estimate for the system. The disagreement highlights concerns over budget projections for the program.

title: IFC Market in China and India is On Cusp of Growth, Valour Predicts
summary: Valour Consultancy predicts significant growth for the In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market in China and India. The report highlights the potential scale of these emerging markets.

title: Cowboy Space Files for 20,000-Satellite Constellation for Data Center Services
summary: Cowboy Space has filed for a 20,000-satellite constellation to provide data center services from space, following a $275 million Series B funding round. The company, formerly known as something else, aims to leverage this massive satellite network for space-based data processing.

title: Iceye Delivers SAR Capability to Polish Armed Forces
summary: Iceye has delivered control over a radar satellite constellation to the Polish Armed Forces for sovereign intelligence. The capability was delivered in less than 12 months.

title: Kymeta Terminals to be Integrated Into Red Cat Uncrewed Surface Vessels
summary: Kymeta is partnering with Red Cat Futures to integrate its satcom terminals into uncrewed surface vessels. The collaboration was announced on May 14.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three major U.S. mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—are forming a joint venture to streamline direct-to-device (D2D) services. This collaboration aims to enhance competition in the D2D space.

title: Intuitive Machines Boosts Deep Space Communications With Goonhilly Acquisition
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired Goonhilly Earth Station, a U.K.-based provider of deep space communication services, to enhance its space communications capabilities. The deal expands Intuitive Machines' ground station network for future lunar and deep space missions.

title: Iridium to Fully Acquire Aireon, Points to Space-Based VHF Opportunity
summary: Iridium is fully acquiring Aireon, its partner operating a space-based air traffic surveillance system. The deal highlights opportunities in space-based VHF technology.

title: Space42 Reports Space Services Revenue Growth Despite Flat Results
summary: Space42 reported flat overall revenues but saw significant growth in its space services revenue for the quarter ending March. The company's performance highlights a contrast between stable total earnings and rising sector-specific income.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch's low-cost Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this effort.
